title: Finding Area by Multiplying Side Lengths

visuals.alt: Grid showing a 4 by 7 rectangle divided into unit squares = 28 sq cm

steps.instruction: A rectangle is 7 cm long and 4 cm wide.
steps.explanation: Length and width are the two dimensions of a rectangle.
Multiplying gives us the number of unit squares that fit inside.

steps.instruction: Area is measured in square units.
steps.explanation: Since we measured in cm, the area is in square centimeters (sq cm).

title: Finding a Missing Side Length from Area

steps.instruction: A rectangle has an area of 42 square inches and a length of 7 inches. Find the width.
steps.explanation: We know two of the three values in Area = length × width.
Division is the opposite of multiplication. If 7 × ? = 42, then ? = 42 ÷ 7.
Always check by multiplying back to confirm.
